initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['transmute']
Prompt family: transmute
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Decompose the provided problem into its fundamental algorithmic components to create a structured technical guide for the next agent. Your guide must follow these rules:
1) Identify the core data structures required (e.g., Tries, Automata, Segment Trees).
2) Define the algorithmic paradigm needed to satisfy the optimization objective (e.g., greedy construction, dynamic programming, or tree traversals).
3) Outline the critical structural properties or constraints that must be maintained (e.g., prefix-free properties, lexicographical requirements).

CRITICAL FORMATTING RULE: Your 'message_for_next_agent' must be strictly plain text. Do not use markdown (no bolding, no headers, no lists with symbols), no code fences, and no LaTeX-style backslashes or mathematical notation. Use only standard alphanumeric characters and basic punctuation.

Do not include specific problem values or names. Focus entirely on the 'how' of the algorithmic implementation.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
